title: Understanding File Permissions
description:
## Reading `ls -l` Permissions

You run `ls -l` and see the following line for a script:

```
-rwxr-xr-- 1 root root 482 Jun 16 10:00 deploy.sh
```

Which `chmod` command would set **exactly** these permissions on `deploy.sh`?
explanation: Permissions are read in three groups of three bits: owner, group, others. `rwx` = 4+2+1 = 7 (owner), `r-x` = 4+0+1 = 5 (group), `r--` = 4+0+0 = 4 (others). So the octal mode is `754`. Option `a` (755) would give others execute too, and `c` (644) removes all execute bits.

hints.title: Octal permission values
hints.body: Each permission group is a sum of read (4), write (2), and execute (1). Add the values within each group to get its digit.
